subject: [PATCH v6 08/15] mm: handle VM_UFFD_RWP in khugepaged, rmap, and GUP

2026-05-29.patch_summary: This patch extends three Linux kernel paths to treat VM_UFFD_RWP (User Faultfd Read-Write Protected) the same as VM_UFFD_WP (User Faultfd Write Protected). The affected paths are khugepaged, rmap, and GUP. In khugepaged, ranges with VM_UFFD_RWP markers are protected from collapse. In rmap, unmap batching is capped at 1 for VM_UFFD_RWP to ensure each PTE can be handled individually. In GUP, faults are forced on VM_UFFD_RWP VMAs regardless of FOLL_HONOR_NUMA_FAULT, as RWP uses protnone as an access-tracking marker rather than a NUMA hinting marker.

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
Gave Reviewed-by

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes suggested replacing the existing condition in gup_can_follow_protnone() with a call to vma_test_single_mask(), which checks for VM_UFFD_RWP, and added a check for vma accessibility

2026-06-03.patch_summary: This patch extends three Linux kernel paths to treat VM_UFFD_RWP (User Faultd Read-Write Protected) the same as VM_UFFD_WP (User Faultd Write Protected). The affected paths are khugepaged, rmap, and GUP. In khugepaged, ranges with the uffd PTE bit are now skipped from collapse. In rmap, batching is capped at 1 for VM_UFFD_RWP to ensure each PTE is handled individually. In GUP, a fault is forced on VM_UFFD_RWP VMAs regardless of FOLL_HONOR_NUMA_FAULT. This patch ensures that per-PTE RWP state is not silently destroyed or bypassed.
